articles.title: Screen Time Rules for Little Kids
articles.summary: The government says kids under five should not watch screens for more than one hour each day. Kids under two should not watch screens alone. Parents should watch screens with their kids and choose slow videos. They can swap screen time for reading books or playing games together. This helps kids sleep better and play more, which is important for growing up. Many kids watch screens every day by age two, but it's better when adults join in. Parents should also show good habits by not using screens too much themselves. The rules might change as we learn more.

articles.title: A Woman Who Lied About Being Sick to Get Money
articles.summary: A woman named Catherine Wieland said she was too sick to leave her house and got over £23,000 in benefits money. But she was not really sick. She went on fun trips like surfing and ziplining in Mexico and visited parks and pubs. She spent the money on things like manicures and tanning. The government found out she was lying. She had to say sorry in court and must pay back all the money. A minister said this was wrong because the money is for people who truly need help. This story teaches us that lying to get benefits is not fair to others.

articles.title: Idaho's New Bathroom Rule for Transgender People
articles.summary: In Idaho, lawmakers are thinking about a new rule that could make it illegal for transgender people to use bathrooms that match their gender identity, even in private businesses like stores or restaurants. This rule would apply to places open to the public. If passed, breaking this rule could lead to jail time, with up to one year for a first offense and up to five years for a second offense. Supporters say it's about safety and privacy in bathrooms, while opponents, including police groups, worry it will be hard to enforce and could lead to unfair treatment. The rule has some exceptions, like for emergencies or people helping children. This is part of a bigger debate in many states about bathroom laws for transgender individuals.

articles.title: Charts Show Trouble for Trump on Iran War
articles.summary: President Trump's popularity is dropping because of the Iran war. Charts show that gas prices have gone up to about $4 a gallon since the war started. This makes people worried about money. Trump's approval rating on the economy fell from 43% to 29%. His overall approval rating also went down from 52% to 40%. The war is causing problems for the global economy and pushing prices higher. This could be bad for Trump in the upcoming elections. Democrats are doing better in some elections because of these issues. People are talking about the elections at a big conservative meeting.

articles.title: Court Says Some Immigrants Can Be Held Without a Chance for Bail
articles.summary: A court in the United States has made a decision about immigrants. It says that the government can keep some immigrants in detention without letting them ask for bail. This means they might have to stay in jail while waiting for their court cases, even if they have not committed any crimes. The court's ruling supports a policy from the Trump administration. Before this, many immigrants without criminal records could ask for bail. Now, this rule might change for people who are not at the border. The case involved a man from Mexico who was arrested in Minneapolis. He wanted a bail hearing, but the court said no. Some judges disagreed, saying this is a new rule that could affect many people. The government is happy with the decision, while groups like the ACLU are concerned.

articles.title: Iran's Warning to U.S. Troops in a Conflict
articles.summary: This article talks about a conflict between Iran and the United States. In Iran's capital, Tehran, there have been airstrikes, which are attacks from planes in the sky. People in Tehran hear loud noises from jets and explosions. The Iranian government says its forces are fighting back against U.S. and Israeli interests. Every night, many Iranians gather in squares to show support for their country's forces. The article also mentions that the U.S. and Iran are talking through messages with help from others, but it's confusing because leaders say different things. Iran warns that if the U.S. sends soldiers on the ground, there might be a surprise for them. This shows tensions are high, and people are watching what happens next.
